title: vicia japonica subsp. pallida
intro: Vicia japonica subsp. pallida, also known as Japanese vetch, is a subspecies of perennial legume native to East Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Vicia japonica subsp. pallida include its potential to reduce blood pressure, alleviate menopausal symptoms, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
